```xml
            <summary>Determines whether two Color values are not equal.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color.ToString">
            <inheritdoc/>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.ColorExtensions">
            <summary>
            Extension methods for <see cref="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color"/>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.ColorExtensions.WithAlpha01(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color,System.Double)">
            <summary>Returns a new Color with the specified alpha value in the range 0.0 to 1.0.</summary>
            <param name="color">The source color.</param>
            <param name="alpha">The new alpha value (0.0 = transparent, 1.0 = opaque).</param>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Colors">
            <summary>
            Provides a set of predefined named colors.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Colors.TransparentBlack">
            <summary>Transparent black (RGBA: 0, 0, 0, 0).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Colors.TransparentWhite">
            <summary>Transparent white (RGBA: 1, 1, 1, 0).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.FontInfo">
            <summary>
            Information about a font: its typeface, default rendering height and ascent.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.FontInfo.#ctor(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Typeface,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Information about a font: its typeface, default rendering height and ascent.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Hash">
            <summary>
            Deterministic pseudo-random hash functions. Same input → same output every
            frame, every render, every device. Useful for placing things like stars,
            foam scatter, glitter etc. without needing a stateful Random.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Hash.From2D(System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Pseudo-random value in [0, 1) from a 2D position. Standard GLSL-style
            hash: <c>fract(sin(dot(p, k)) * 43758.5453)</c>.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Hash.From3D(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Pseudo-random value in [0, 1) from a 3D position (use for x,y plus a
            time slice or seed dimension).
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Hash.From1D(System.Double)">
            <summary>Pseudo-random value in [0, 1) from a single integer index.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Interpolation">
            <summary>
            Controls how pixels are sampled when scaling or transforming layers.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Interpolation.NearestNeighbor">
            <summary>
            Sharp, blocky pixels — ideal for pixel art on small displays.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Interpolation.Linear">
            <summary>
            Smooth, blended pixels — bilinear interpolation for softer results.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH">
            <summary>
            Small math helpers that show up in almost every pixogram.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.Clamp01(System.Double)">
            <summary>Clamps a value to [0, 1].</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.Clamp(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Clamps a value to [<paramref name="min"/>, <paramref name="max"/>].</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.Lerp(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Linear interpolation: returns a + (b-a)*t.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.LerpClamped(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Linear interpolation, clamped to t ∈ [0, 1].</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.Smoothstep(System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Smoothstep curve (Hermite interpolation): t² * (3 - 2t).
            Input clamped to [0, 1]. Slow start AND slow end, fast in the middle.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.Smootherstep(System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Smootherstep curve (Perlin's 6t⁵ - 15t⁴ + 10t³).
            Even smoother first and second derivatives than Smoothstep.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.Remap(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Maps v from [inMin, inMax] linearly to [outMin, outMax]. NOT clamped.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.MathH.RemapClamped(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Maps v from [inMin, inMax] linearly to [outMin, outMax], clamped to the output range.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paint">
            <summary>
            Represents a paint that can be used for filling or stroking shapes.
            Can be a solid color, gradient, pattern, or custom shader.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.SolidPaint">
            <summary>
            A solid color paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.SolidPaint.#ctor(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color)">
            <summary>
            A solid color paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.LinearGradientPaint">
            <summary>
            A linear gradient paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.RadialGradientPaint">
            <summary>
            A radial gradient paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TwoPointConicalGradientPaint">
            <summary>
            A two-point conical gradient paint (cone gradient between two circles).
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.SweepGradientPaint">
            <summary>
            A sweep (angular) gradient paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.PerlinNoiseFractalPaint">
            <summary>
            A Perlin noise fractal pattern paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.PerlinNoiseTurbulencePaint">
            <summary>
            A Perlin noise turbulence pattern paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.ImagePatternPaint">
            <summary>
            An image-based pattern/texture paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints">
            <summary>
            Factory methods for creating paints.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.Solid(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color)">
            <summary>
            Creates a solid color paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.LinearGradient(System.ValueTuple{System.Double,System.Double},System.ValueTuple{System.Double,System.Double},Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[],System.Double[],Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode)">
            <summary>
            Creates a linear gradient paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.VerticalGradient(System.Double,Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[])">
            <summary>
            Creates a vertical gradient paint (top to bottom).
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.HorizontalGradient(System.Double,Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[])">
            <summary>
            Creates a horizontal gradient paint (left to right).
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.RadialGradient(System.ValueTuple{System.Double,System.Double},System.Double,Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[],System.Double[],Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode)">
            <summary>
            Creates a radial gradient paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.TwoPointConicalGradient(System.ValueTuple{System.Double,System.Double},System.Double,System.ValueTuple{System.Double,System.Double},System.Double,Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[],System.Double[],Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode)">
            <summary>
            Creates a two-point conical gradient paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.SweepGradient(System.ValueTuple{System.Double,System.Double},Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[],System.Double[],System.Nullable{Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D})">
            <summary>
            Creates a sweep (angular) gradient paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.PerlinNoiseFractal(System.Double,System.Double,System.Int32,System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Creates a Perlin noise fractal pattern paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.PerlinNoiseTurbulence(System.Double,System.Double,System.Int32,System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Creates a Perlin noise turbulence pattern paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Paints.ImagePattern(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.SingleImage,Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode,Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode,System.Nullable{Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D})">
            <summary>
            Creates an image-based pattern/texture paint.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette">
             <summary>
             An ordered set of colour stops sampled by a normalised position t ∈ [0, 1].
            
             Use a <see cref="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette"/> when you need a colour-over-time or colour-over-
             value lookup — e.g. health bars, time-of-day tints, value-mapped heatmaps,
             fire-style flame gradients. Compare with <c>Paints.LinearGradient</c>, which
             fills a 2D shape with a gradient; <see cref="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette"/> returns a single
             <see cref="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color"/> per call.
             </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette.Colors">
            <summary>The colour stops, in order.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette.Stops">
            <summary>
            The normalised positions [0..1] of each colour stop. If you constructed
            the palette without explicit positions, stops are evenly spaced.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette.#ctor(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[])">
            <summary>
            Creates a palette with evenly-spaced stops between the given colours.
            Two colours → t=0 and t=1; three colours → t=0, 0.5, 1; etc.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette.#ctor(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Color[],System.Double[])">
            <summary>
            Creates a palette with explicit stop positions. <paramref name="stops"/>
            must be the same length as <paramref name="colors"/>, sorted ascending,
            and within [0, 1].
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette.Sample(System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Samples the palette at position <paramref name="t"/> ∈ [0, 1].
            Returns the colour interpolated between the surrounding stops.
            Values outside [0, 1] clamp to the first/last colour.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palette.SampleWrapped(System.Double)">
            <summary>
            Samples the palette wrapping around — values outside [0, 1] wrap to a
            repeating cycle. Useful for periodic effects (rotating hue, looping fire).
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes">
            <summary>
            Built-in palettes for common use-cases.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes.Rainbow">
            <summary>Full-spectrum rainbow (red → yellow → green → cyan → blue → magenta → red).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes.Heat">
            <summary>Heatmap: cool blue → green → yellow → red.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes.Fire">
            <summary>Fire/lava: black → red → orange → yellow → white.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes.Ice">
            <summary>Ice/glacier: deep blue → ice blue → frosted white.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes.Sunset">
            <summary>Sunset / golden hour: deep violet → magenta → orange → gold → pale yellow.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes.Grayscale">
            <summary>Greyscale ramp from black to white.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Palettes.Phosphor">
            <summary>Classic CRT phosphor green (black → dark green → bright green).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextEdging">
            <summary>
            Controls how glyph edges are rendered.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextEdging.Pixel">
            <summary>
            Hard pixel edges — no anti-aliasing. Best for crisp pixel-art rendering
            on the 24×24 canvas (this is the default).
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextEdging.AntiAlias">
            <summary>Smooth anti-aliased edges.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextEdging.SubpixelAntiAlias">
            <summary>Subpixel anti-aliasing (designed for LCD-style colour fringing).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextHinting">
            <summary>
            Controls how aggressively glyphs are snapped to the pixel grid.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextHinting.None">
            <summary>No hinting (default — keeps pixel-art glyphs unmodified).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextHinting.Slight">
            <summary>Light hinting — minor pixel-grid alignment.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextHinting.Normal">
            <summary>Normal hinting — standard alignment for legibility.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TextHinting.Full">
            <summary>Full hinting — strongest alignment, may distort glyph shapes.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode">
            <summary>
            Controls how a paint repeats outside its primary domain (e.g., outside a
            linear gradient's start/end range, or outside an image pattern's bounds).
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode.Clamp">
            <summary>Repeat the edge color (default for gradients).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode.Repeat">
            <summary>Tile the pattern, repeating end to start.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode.Mirror">
            <summary>Tile the pattern, mirroring every other repetition.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="F:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.TileMode.Decal">
            <summary>Render only inside the primary domain; outside is transparent.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D">
             <summary>
             A 2D affine transform (translation, scale, rotation, skew). Used to
             transform paint coordinates (e.g. to rotate a gradient or tile an image
             at an angle).
            
             Transforms compose left-to-right via <see cref="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Then(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D)"/>:
             <c>Transform2D.Translate(5, 0).Then(Transform2D.Rotate(45))</c> first
             translates, then rotates.
             </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Identity">
            <summary>The identity transform (no change).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Translate(System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Creates a translation transform.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Scale(System.Double)">
            <summary>Creates a uniform scale transform around the origin.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Scale(System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Creates a non-uniform scale transform around the origin.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Rotate(System.Double)">
            <summary>Creates a rotation transform around the origin (in degrees).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Rotate(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Creates a rotation transform around a given pivot (in degrees).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Skew(System.Double,System.Double)">
            <summary>Creates a skew transform (kx, ky in tangent units).</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="M:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D.Then(Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Transform2D)">
            <summary>Composes this transform with another, applied AFTER this one.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Typeface">
            <summary>
            An opaque handle to a loaded typeface. Pixogram authors don't construct
            these directly — use the predefined <see cref="T:Pxl.Ui.CSharp.Fonts"/> entries.
            </summary>
        </member>
    </members>
</doc>
```
<!-- XML-DOCS:END -->
